key_insight: PI-MFA reconstructs continuous flow fields with B-splines while enforcing PDE, initial, and boundary constraints so the result is both smooth and physically consistent.
engineering_relevance: This gives engineers a practical way to generate differentiable surrogate fields for analysis, visualization, and downstream computations without sacrificing governing-law fidelity.
method: Tensor-product B-splines build compact, continuously differentiable field representations.
Control points are solved by optimization that balances data fit with PDE residuals and IBCs.
Exact spline derivatives are used to evaluate residuals efficiently without storing full-resolution fields.
evidence_signal: Across 1D convection-diffusion, 2D coupled Burgers, and 2D incompressible Navier-Stokes tests, PI-MFA lowered PDE residuals and improved balance-law consistency, while also outperforming standard/regularized MFA on physically inconsistent data and showing advantages over tested PINNs.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality of the chosen physics model and constraint weighting; overly strict or mismatched residual terms may bias the reconstruction.
paper_title: A Physics-Informed B-Spline Framework for Continuous Approximation of Flow Data
